answer:
The Nyquist sampling rate for a 5 kHz signal is 10 kHz, so the sampling period is \( T_s = 1/10{,}000 = 1 \times 10^{-4} \) seconds. With 2000 signals time-division multiplexed, the maximum pulse duration is \( \tau_{\text{max}} = T_s / 2000 = 5 \times 10^{-8} \) seconds. This matches option E.  

\boxed{E}
